title: Coniogramme glabra
taxonomy: Coniogramme glabra is classified as a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. It belongs to the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. This classification places Coniogramme glabra within the division Pteridophyta. The family Pteridaceae is a large and diverse group of ferns commonly known as the linden-moss or linden-moss family. Coniogramme glabra is a unique and fascinating member of this family.

In traditional Chinese medicine, it is valued for its ability to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. intro: Coniogramme glabra, commonly known as the smooth coniogramme, is a species of fern native to the tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Coniogramme glabra include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared into tea, infusions, or decoctions, or used in the form of capsules or tablets to leverage its health benefits.
